questions.q: Which statement is true in any field $F$?
choices: Every nonzero element has a multiplicative inverse.
questions.explain: A field requires that $F^{\times}=F\setminus\{0\}$ is a group under multiplication, so each nonzero element is invertible.

questions.q: In the additive group $(\mathbb{Z}/12\mathbb{Z},+)$, what is the order of the element $4$?
Which structure models symmetry most directly?
questions.explain: Symmetries compose and invert, exactly the group axioms.

questions.q: In a Cayley table for a finite group $G$, each row is a permutation of $G$ primarily because of:
choices: Left cancellation (equivalently, left multiplication is bijective)
questions.explain: For fixed $g$, the map $h\mapsto gh$ is bijective with inverse $h\mapsto g^{-1}h$, so each row lists each element once.

questions.q: If $|G|=24$ and $|H|=6$, how many left cosets does $H$ have in $G$?
A subgroup $H\le G$ is normal exactly when:
questions.explain: Normality means left and right cosets coincide for every group element.

questions.q: The First Isomorphism Theorem says for a homomorphism $\varphi:G\to H$:
questions.explain: Quotienting by the kernel identifies exactly elements with equal image.
A finite field $\mathbb{F}_{p^n}$ has exactly $p^n$ elements.

questions.q: In characteristic $p$, the Frobenius map on $\mathbb{F}_{p^n}$ is:
questions.explain: The Frobenius endomorphism raises each element to the $p$th power.
